zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Scrope於大約1420年出生在英國越可爾馬斯哈姆時，她的父親Sir John Scrope34歲，母親Elizabeth Chaworth30歲。她於1435年與Henry Scrope在英國英格蘭越可爾馬斯哈姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1498年8月29日在英國越可爾博爾頓享年79歲，埋葬在英國越可爾易亞斯比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Scrope于大约1420年出生在英国越可爾馬斯哈姆时，她的父亲Sir John Scrope34岁，母亲Elizabeth Chaworth30岁。她于1435年与Henry Scrope在英国英格兰越可爾馬斯哈姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1498年8月29日在英国越可爾博爾頓享年79岁，埋葬在英国越可爾易亞斯比。

nameMeanings.name: Scrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: of uncertain origin. Tengvik suggests derivation from the Old Scandinavian personal name Skrópi (of doubtful etymology, but compare Old Scandinavian skrópa-maðr ‘hypocrite’, containing the genitive plural form of skrópar ‘sham, hypocrisy’). It is possible, but not certain, that this personal name forms the first element of the Notts place-name Scrooby. Otherwise there is no evidence for its currency in England or, more relevantly, in Normandy. Richards Castle (in Herefs and Shrops) was apparently named after Richard Scrope, who held estates in Herefs and Worcs in 1066 and was a Norman favourite of Edward the Confessor. The Scrope family, whose name is traditionally pronounced /skru:p/, was politically important in medieval England, and acquired estates in Lincs (Barton on Humber), NR Yorks (Castle Bolton), and in ER Yorks (Flotmanby). Richard (le) Scrope of Bolton, in Wensleydale, was Lord Chancellor in the reign of Richard II. The use of le and de in the surname, suggesting either a nickname or a locative name, must be treated with caution because these were often added gratuitously to Anglo-Norman surnames as a sign of high social status.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Scrope was born about 1420, in Masham, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir John Scrope, was 34 and her mother, Elizabeth Chaworth, was 30. She married Henry Scrope in 1435, in Masham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 29 August 1498, in Bolton, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 79, and was buried in Easby, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Scrope est née environ 1420, à Masham, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Scrope, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Chaworth, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Henry Scrope en 1435, à Masham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 août 1498, à Bolton, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Easby, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Scropeが約1420年にイギリスヨークシュアイアマシャムで生まれた時，父Sir John Scropeは34歳，母Elizabeth Chaworthは30歳であった。1435年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアマシャムでHenry Scropeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1498年8月29日にイギリスヨークシュアイアボルトンで79歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアエアスビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Scrope(이)가 대략 1420년 영국 요르크실르 마샤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Scrope(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Chaworth(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1435년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 마샤에서 Henry Scrope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1498년 8월 29일 영국 요르크실르 볼턴에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 이스비에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Scrope nasceu aproximadamente 1420, em Masham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Scrope, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Chaworth, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry Scrope em 1435, em Masham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de agosto de 1498, em Bolton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 79 anos, e foi sepultada em Easby, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Scrope родилась около 1420 г. в местности машам, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Scrope было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Chaworth, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Scrope в 1435 г. в местности машам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 29 августа 1498 г. в местности болтон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности эасби, еркшир, Англия.
